title: Coryphantha cornuta
intro: Coryphantha cornuta, also known as the "horned pincushion cactus," is a species of cactus that belongs to the genus Coryphantha. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coryphantha cornuta include glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coryphantha cornuta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Coryphantha cornuta is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. This species is also known as the "horned pincushion" due to its unique appearance. Coryphantha cornuta is further classified within the genus Coryphantha, which comprises a group of small to medium-sized cacti.
